I therefore propose that we divide into three parties, the first of which shall be posted at the Chimneys, the second at the mouth of the Mercy. As to the third, I think it would be best to place it on the islet, so as to prevent, or at all events delay, any attempt at landing. We have the use of two rifles and four muskets. Each of us will be armed, and, as we are amply provided with powder and shot, we need not spare our fire. We have nothing to fear from the muskets, nor even from the guns of the brig. Nemo's Electricity is the single power source behind the [[Nautilus]] — the light, heat, and motive force of [[captain-nemo|Captain Nemo]]'s submarine, all drawn from the sea itself. When Professor Aronnax marvels that everything aboard runs without coal or contact with the shore, [[captain-nemo|Nemo]] answers with the principle on which his whole independence rests. (TTLU-012-011)(TTLU-012-023)

This electricity is the engine of [[captain-nemo|Nemo]]'s freedom: it severs every dependence on the land and makes the [[nautilus|Nautilus]] a wholly self-contained world. The long exposition to Aronnax is Verne's device for establishing that the submarine's wonders rest on a stated, if generous, physical basis rather than pure fantasy.

The Go-Ahead is Verne's vehicle for the novel's central debate: lighter-than-air versus heavier-than-air flight. The [[Weldon Institute]] insisted that the true atmospheric vehicle was the aerostat and dismissed the inventions of rival aviators. (RC-022-006)

It later proves central to the colony's engineering: to raise the water level they need only "establish a dam at the two openings made by the lake, and by which were fed Creek Glycerine and Falls River." (MI-048-006)

An island-continent of the southern seas, Australia is crossed by Glenarvan's party along the 37th parallel through the province of Victoria — a traverse from the eastern coast that forms the whole central movement of the search.

Crete, also called Candia, is the ancient island toward which the [[Nautilus]] once steered. (TTLU-030-072) Among Captain Nemo's crew the narrator thought he recognized a native of either Greece or Crete. (TTLU-018-005)
